news.headline: Dow Jones drops 299 points as Fed official comments on interest rates, Nvidia rises after Wall Street call.
news.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average plunged by 299 points amidst growth in other market indexes. A prominent Federal Reserve official made remarks on interest rates. Nvidia experienced a significant rally in today's stock market session.

news.headline: AI company OpenAI faces new lawsuit as it partners with Time for undisclosed sum.
Crypto market surges as spot BTC ETF inflows rise and bullish investors return, boosting prices.
news.summary: Today, the cryptocurrency market has seen a rise in value due to increased inflows in spot Bitcoin Exchange-Traded Fund (ETF) and a favorable technical setup. These factors have attracted the attention of bullish investors, leading to a positive trading sentiment. The market has experienced a significant increase in both value and trading activity as a result.

news.headline: NASA awards $476.5M contract to Leidos, Inc. for mission support including ISS Program and Artemis campaign.
news.summary: In Washington on June 27, 2024, NASA granted a contract to Leidos, Inc. located in Reston, Virginia. This agreement involves offering assistance for NASA's International Space Station Program, Artemis campaign, and other related missions. The contract known as Cargo Mission Contract 4 is valued at a total potential of $476.5 million.

news.headline: Metro West Chapter of Credit Unions in Michigan offers grants up to $2,500 for non-profits to support local community activities.
news.summary: The Metro West Chapter of Credit Unions in Michigan is currently offering grants of up to $2,500 for non-profit organizations in Livonia. These grants are intended to support community improvement initiatives. Interested organizations are encouraged to apply for the funding by the specified deadline.

news.headline: Ensurge Micropower ASA cancels subsequent offering.
Nokia is set to acquire Infinera, boosting its presence in Optical Networks and advancing its product roadmap.
SC rejects plan to shield Sacklers in Purdue Pharma deal, jeopardizing it. Family members face civil claims related to opioid crisis.
news.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed a bankruptcy deal that aimed to shield Sackler family members from liability in opioid crisis lawsuits. The maneuver, if accepted, would have safeguarded family members from civil claims stemming from the epidemic. The court's decision has significant implications as it directly impacts the Sackler family's ability to avoid facing legal consequences related to the crisis.

news.headline: Biden and Trump to face off in the first round of debates.
Could Biden and Trump spar over crypto tonight? CNN's Thursday debate may put Bitcoin and digital assets in the spotlight.
news.summary: The upcoming presidential debate on Thursday, hosted by CNN, has the potential to bring Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies to the forefront of political discussion, potentially reaching a broad audience.

news.headline: Live Oak Bancshares names Michael J. Cairns as Chief Credit Officer on Aug 1, 2024, succeeding Steven J. Smits who served for 9 years. Smits moves to head commercial banking.
news.summary: In Wilmington, North Carolina, on June 27, 2024, Live Oak Bancshares revealed Michael J. Cairns as Chief Credit Officer starting from August 1, 2024. Cairns will hold this position for Live Oak Bancshares and Live Oak Bank, taking over from Steven J. Smits, who held the role for 9 years. Smits will transition to a new position as head of commercial banking.

news.headline: Aruza Pest Control acquires Evergreen Pest Solutions operations in Charlotte; focuses on pest, termite, and mosquito control in multiple states.
news.summary: A pest control company called Aruza Pest Control Holdings, based in Charlotte, N.C, announced on June 27, 2024, that it specializes in providing pest, termite, and mosquito control services for residential and commercial properties. Aruza operates primarily in North and South Carolina, Florida, and Texas. It is regarded as a top player in the industry. The company is owned by Concentric Equity, and it serves a significant number of customers across its operational states.

news.headline: Global electric vehicle test equipment market to grow by USD 212.5 million from 2024-2028, with a CAGR of 30.08%.EV industry boost.
news.summary: As of June 27, 2024, the global market size for electric vehicle test equipment is projected to increase by approximately USD 212.5 million between 2024 and 2028, as reported by Technavio. It is expected to maintain a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 30.08% throughout the forecast period. This growth is attributed to the expanding electric vehicle automotive sector...

news.headline: S&P Global appoints Martina L. Cheung as President & CEO, succeeding Douglas L. Peterson who will retire on Nov 1, 2024. Peterson to be a special advisor until Dec 31, 2025. NY, June 27, 2024. NYSE: SPGI.
news.summary: Martina L. Cheung has been named the new President and Chief Executive Officer of the company, while current CEO Douglas L. Peterson will retire. The transition in leadership is set to take place on November 1, 2024. Mr. Peterson will continue to serve as a special advisor to the company until December 31, 2025. The announcement was made on June 27, 2024, in New York. S&P Global, listed as SPGI on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), shared this news.

news.headline: Nike stock dropped by X% after executives cited difficulties with demand, impacting company performance.
JPMorgan analyst reacts to US bank stress test results; significant surprise with increased stress capital buffers for large-cap banks. Capital requirements likely to delay future investment plans.
news.summary: A banking industry expert at JPMorgan has shared insights on the recent announcement of the Federal Reserve’s stress test results, which evaluate the financial stability of U.S. banks through analyzing potential losses, revenues, expenses, and capital levels in different economic scenarios. The results were disclosed on Wednesday.Analyst Vivek Juneja summarized the stress test outcomes for major banks, remarking that they were more surprising than anticipated. Most large-cap banks saw a significant rise in their stress capital buffers (SCB), consequently leading to an increase in their capital requirements. This notable growth in capital requirements is likely to affect the timing or extent of long-term capital return strategies.Juneja noted that all banks assessed surpassed their minimum necessities and are poised to gain from further accumulation of OCI (Other Comprehensive Income) losses. Nevertheless, it is expected that money center banks in the sector will experience delays or revisions in their capital return plans due to the augmented capital requirements.For more detailed information, the full story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Fly-E Group, Inc. completes over-allotment option selling 337,500 extra shares at $4 each, totaling $10.35M gross proceeds.
news.summary: New York, N.Y., June 27, 2024 - Fly-E Group, Inc. (Nasdaq: FLYE) shared that the underwriter for its initial public offering exercised its over-allotment option fully, acquiring an extra 337,500 shares of FLYE's common stock at US$4.00 each. Including this option, FLYE has now sold a total of 2,587,500 shares at US$10.35 million in gross proceeds, prior to deductions for underwriter discounts, commissions, and related expenses. The company's common stock began trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol “FLYE” on June 6, 2024.

news.headline: American Tower Corp (NYSE: AMT) to release Q2 2024 results on July 30, 2024. Conference call at 8:30 am ET, dial (877) 692-8955.
news.summary: Boston-based American Tower Corporation (NYSE: AMT) has revealed that their second-quarter 2024 results will be announced at 7:00 a.m. ET on Tuesday, July 30, 2024. A conference call to discuss these results is set for 8:30 a.m. ET on the same day. Interested parties can join the call by dialing (877) 692-8955 within the U.S./Canada.

news.headline: Sonsray's Force for Good Initiative promotes community involvement by allowing employees a day off to volunteer.
news.summary: Located in Torrance, California, Sonsray demonstrates its commitment to people and the community through the Force for Good program. This initiative allows employees to dedicate a day to volunteering with a charity of their preference.

news.headline: BrightView Holdings increases receivables financing facility from $275M to $325M, extending maturity to June 2027.
news.summary: In Blue Bell, Pennsylvania, BrightView Holdings, Inc. revealed that its subsidiary, BrightView Funding LLC, has augmented its receivables financing agreement from $275 million to $325 million. This extension lasts till June 2027, offering the company a flexible and cost-effective debt option. The Receivables Facility also encompasses an "accordion feature" for further expansion.

news.headline: CCC Intelligent Solutions plans to offer 30 million shares of common stock in a secondary offering by affiliates of Advent International and Oak Hill Capital Partners (NASDAQ: CCCS).
news.summary: In Chicago, CCC Intelligent Solutions Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: CCCS) disclosed plans for a secondary offering involving 30 million shares of the company's common stock. The offering will be conducted by affiliates of Advent International, L.P. and Oak Hill Capital Partners, collectively known as the Selling Stockholders. These shares will be made available for sale through negotiated transactions or at prevailing market prices. The announcement did not specify the full details of the offering beyond the total number of shares involved.

news.headline: AFC Gamma sets July 8, 2024 as record date for Sunrise Realty Trust spin-off. $0.15/share special dividend declared by Board.
news.summary: Sunrise Realty Trust is set to distribute shares on July 8, 2024, with an expected distribution date of July 9, 2024. The Board of Directors has announced a special dividend of $0.15 per share for the spin-off.

news.headline: NamSys Inc., a cash technology provider, reported Q2 revenue of $1,660,620 (9% increase) and net income of $523,928 (28% rise).
news.summary: On June 27, 2024, NamSys Inc. (TSXV:CTZ), a key player in cash processing technology, released their financial results for the second quarter of fiscal 2024 ending on April 30th. Figures are presented in Canadian dollars. In this quarter:- Revenue stood at $1,660,620, showing a 9% increase from $1,519,788 in the same period last year.- Net income rose by 28% to $523,928 ($0.02 per share) compared to $410,917 ($0.02 per share) in the previous year.- Additionally, the Company repurchased and annulled a certain number of shares... (the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com)

news.headline: Accolade Inc stock falls after weak Q1 earnings and guidance: $110.47 million revenue (beat $105.19 million), EPS loss of 35 cents (beat loss of 48 cents).
news.summary: Accolade Inc (NASDAQ:ACCD) saw a significant drop in its shares during after-hours trading on Thursday following the release of its financial results for the fiscal first quarter and the issuance of a disappointing guidance. The company reported Q1 revenue of $110.47 million, surpassing estimates of $105.19 million. Additionally, they posted a Q1 EPS of a loss of 35 cents, which beat estimates for a loss of 48 cents. Accolade Inc expressed their commitment to advancing in Healthcare AI and maintaining a successful engagement model to sustain and increase profitability. Their primary objective remains focused on providing exceptional member experiences and implementing strategic plans for future growth.

news.headline: Nike reported Q4 revenue of $12.6B, missing estimates. Nike Direct revenue was $5.1B, down 8%, while Wholesale revenue was $7.1B, up 5%. Converse revenue was $480M, down 18% YoY. APAC showed growth, offset by North America. Gross margin rose to 44.7%.
news.summary: Nike Inc reported its fourth-quarter financial results with revenue totaling $12.6 billion, a decrease of 2% compared to the previous year. Despite missing the Street consensus estimate of $12.91 billion, Nike Direct revenue reached $5.1 billion in Q4, a decrease of 8%, while Wholesale revenues saw an increase to $7.1 billion, up 5% year-over-year. Converse revenue was $480 million, down 18% year-over-year. Nike experienced growth in regions like China, although this was offset by weaker performance in North America. The company's gross margin increased by 110 basis points to 44.7% in the fourth quarter.

news.headline: Patricia Chiodo joins Knife River Corp board with financial expertise. Ex-CFO of Verra Mobility and RSC Holdings.
news.summary: BISMARCK, N.D.--In recent news, Knife River Corporation (NYSE: KNF) shared the appointment of Patricia Chiodo to their board of directors. With a background rich in financial knowledge, Chiodo is set to contribute to the company's Audit and Compensation committees. Formerly the CFO of Verra Mobility Corporation until her retirement in 2022, her previous role was CFO at RSC Holdings, a company specializing in renting construction and industrial equipment. Chiodo brings a wealth of experience to her new position on the board.

news.headline: Four Seasons Education files annual report on Form 20-F for Fiscal Year 2024 in Shanghai on June 27, 2024.
news.summary: Announced in Shanghai on June 27, 2024, Four Seasons Education (Cayman) Inc. ("Four Seasons Education" or "the Company") (NYSE: FEDU), a China-based tourism and education service provider, filed its annual report on Form 20-F for the fiscal year.

news.headline: Carvana showcases efficient operations tech, infra at Rocklin IRC to investors and analysts. "CVNA’s competitive moat" - J.P. Morgan.

The focus was on showcasing the technology and processes powering Carvana's reconditioning and fulfillment operations. J.P. Morgan summarized the event, stating, "We believe CVNA’s competitive advantage was evident."

news.headline: Faraday Future (Nasdaq: FFIE) approved for Nasdaq listing, must comply with reporting by July 31, 2024.

The Nasdaq Hearings Panel has approved the company's appeal for ongoing listing on Nasdaq, contingent on meeting the periodic reporting obligation by July 31, 2024. Faraday Future is a California-based firm focusing on a global shared intelligent electric mobility ecosystem.

news.headline: Benson Hill receives non-binding offer from Argonautic to acquire all outstanding shares of common stock.
news.summary: A preliminary, non-binding offer has been presented to Benson Hill, Inc. to acquire all outstanding shares of common stock. The offer is currently under consideration in St. Louis.

Interested parties can access the webcast link and presentation material on the Investor Relations page of the company's website at http://ir.murphyoilcorp.com.

news.headline: KittyHawk appoints Alexandra Peterson as new Partner, bringing extensive experience to tech investment firm.
news.summary: On June 27, 2024, in Los Angeles, KittyHawk, a technology investment firm focusing on backing innovative seed to late-stage pre-IPO companies, welcomed Alexandra Peterson as a Partner. With this appointment, Alexandra brings her wealth of experience and expertise to the firm.

news.headline: AZZ Inc. declares $0.17/share cash dividend for Q1 fiscal year 2025; Continues leading with galvanizing, coil coating.
news.summary: In Fort Worth, Texas on June 27, 2024, AZZ Inc., a major player in hot-dip galvanizing and coil coating services, disclosed that its Board of Directors approved a cash dividend of $0.17 per share for the first quarter. AZZ Inc. is listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol AZZ.

news.headline: Carisma Therapeutics nominates first CAR-M candidate for hepatocellular carcinoma under Moderna collaboration, triggering $2M payment.
news.summary: A potential treatment candidate focusing on Glypican-3 for solid tumors, such as hepatocellular carcinoma, has been identified. This nomination has led to a $2 million milestone payment being awarded to Carisma. This update comes from Carisma Therapeutics Inc., listed as Nasdaq: CARM, based in Philadelphia. The news was announced on June 27, 2024.

news.headline: Ball Corp (NYSE: BALL) to announce Q2 earnings on Aug 1, 2024, before NYSE trading begins. Conference at 9 am MT/11 am ET.
news.summary: Ball Corporation (NYSE: BALL) is scheduled to disclose its financial results for the second quarter of 2024 on Thursday, August 1, 2024. The announcement will be made before the start of trading on the New York Stock Exchange. The earnings report will be released at 9 a.m. Mountain time, which is 11 a.m. Eastern time. This is an important update for investors and analysts tracking the company's performance.

news.headline: GoDaddy Inc. (NYSE: GDDY) appoints Graham Smith as new independent director on June 26, 2024.
news.summary: On June 27, 2024, in Tempe, Arizona, GoDaddy Inc. (NYSE: GDDY) announced the appointment of Graham Smith as a new independent director. This decision by the board of directors was unanimously approved and is effective as of June 26, 2024. Smith will serve as a Class I director until the company's 2025 annual meeting of stockholders.

news.summary: Aircastle Limited, based in Stamford, Connecticut, is set to unveil its financial results for the first quarter ending on May 31, 2024, on July 11, 2024, before the market opens. Management will be releasing the details in a press release on that day.

news.headline: Global exfoliators and scrubs market to grow by USD 658.7 million by 2028 at 4.36% CAGR due to demand for skin-lightening products.
news.summary: As of June 27, 2024, the worldwide exfoliators and scrubs market is projected to expand by approximately USD 658.7 million between 2024 and 2028, as per findings from Technavio. Throughout the forecast period, the market is expected to experience a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 4.36%. This growth is driven by an increasing interest in skin-lightening products and treatments.

news.headline: The Federal Reserve issued $7.03 billion in discount-window loans in the week ending June 26, up from $6.68 billion the week before.
GXO and Agility Robotics sign agreement for Robots-as-a-Service at SPANX facility, integrating Humanoid robots - industry-first deal.
news.summary: A recent partnership involving Robots-as-a-Service (RaaS) has seen Agility’s Humanoid robots being integrated with other collaborative robots (cobots) at a SPANX facility. This agreement aims to maximize efficiency and productivity by combining the unique capabilities of these robots. The collaboration will enable the facility to benefit from the diverse skills and functionalities brought by the robots. This move underscores the increasing trend of automation in various industries.

news.headline: Ingersoll Rand unveils its 2023 Sustainability Report showcasing accelerated progress and new goals for sustainability. #IR #Sustainability #Goals
news.summary: In Davidson, North Carolina on June 27, 2024, Ingersoll Rand Inc. (NYSE: IR), a worldwide supplier of critical flow creation and solutions for life science and industrial sectors, has released its 2023 Sustainability Report titled “Leading Sustainably through execution.” The report outlines the company's dedication, steps taken, and achievements towards a more sustainable future. The approach is steered by Ingersoll Rand Execution Excellence (IRX) and a mindset of ownership, demonstrating the company's commitment to sustainability strategies.

news.headline: SC rejects move to shield Sacklers from civil claims in opioid crisis, jeopardizing deal.
news.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed a bankruptcy agreement that sought to shield the Sackler family from lawsuits tied to the opioid crisis. The move means that members of the Sackler family cannot evade civil claims linked to the epidemic.

news.headline: $106.6 billion in term funding loans from Fed bank in week ended June 26th, down from $107.3 billion prior week.
iAnthus reports results from Annual General Meeting. Company owns cannabis operations in US. (Characters: 87)
news.summary: On June 27, 2024, iAnthus Capital Holdings, Inc. (CSE: IAN) (OTCQB: ITHUF), a company involved in regulated cannabis operations in the US, announced its results for the Annual General meeting.

news.headline: SITE Centers Corp. released its 2023 Corporate Sustainability Report, the 10th overall, following GRI Standards.
news.summary: In Beachwood, Ohio, SITE Centers Corp. (NYSE:SITC), a company that owns open-air shopping centers in affluent suburban areas, has unveiled its 2023 Corporate Sustainability Report (CSR). This marks their tenth sustainability report, with eight of them aligning with the Global Reporting Initiative (GRI) Standards. The report offers insight into SITE Centers’ corporate responsibility and sustainability initiatives.

news.headline: Pure Strategies launches Pure Supply™ platform for supply chain impact; enhances sustainability programs for brands & retailers.
news.summary: A sustainability consulting firm based in Gloucester, Massachusetts, recently announced their efforts in assisting companies to enhance their approach in engaging suppliers. Pure Strategies is dedicated to helping brands and retailers maximize their supplier engagement strategies to further their climate goals and sustainability initiatives. The consultancy aims to collaborate with businesses to strengthen their sustainability programs. This update was disclosed on June 27, 2024.

news.headline: Actelis secures $300,000+ order for Italian transportation project, boosting international growth.
news.summary: Actelis has confirmed a recent order exceeding $300,000 for the backing of a crucial national transportation infrastructure endeavor in Italy.

news.headline: Chewy shares surge after Roaring Kitty tweet boosts pet-care sector, impacting stocks on Thursday.
news.summary: On Thursday, Roaring Kitty (also known as Keith Gill) demonstrated the ability to impact the stock prices of companies beyond GameStop, such as Chewy and other pet-care companies.

news.headline: Cryptocurrency markets see gains before U.S. debate; Bitcoin at $61,464.37 (+1%), Eth $3,449.60 (+2%), Solana $148.18 (+8.5%), Dogecoin $0.1244 (+0.6%). Large transactions up 15.9%, but netflows down 196.5%. $91.8M liquidated in 24hrs, BlackRock buys Bitcoin.
news.summary: Cryptocurrency markets are experiencing an increase in prices leading up to the Trump-Biden U.S. Presidential election debate, with the main catalyst being the Solana spot ETF application. The current prices for the top cryptocurrencies are as follows: Bitcoin stands at $61,464.37, showing a 1% gain, Ethereum is at $3,449.60 with a 2% increase, Solana is at $148.18 displaying an 8.5% climb, Dogecoin is at $0.1244 with a 0.6% rise, and Shiba Inu is at $0.0000175 with a 1.5% uptick. Notable statistics reveal a 15.9% surge in large transaction volume, alongside a 196.5% decrease in exchange netflows in a single day. Coinglass data indicates that 34,732 traders were liquidated, resulting in losses totaling $91.8 million within the past 24 hours. Additionally, Radar data shows that BlackRock has acquired Bitcoin for its Global Allocation Fund based on an SEC filing, making it the third internal fund in BlackRock to invest in Bitcoin. (Note: Full story available on Benzinga.com)
